Scenario.overview: Telecommunications Industry Complex A sophisticated cyber attack orchestrated by the expert-level threat group C0027 targets the telecommunications sector. The attackers employ a multi-stage approach, starting with reconnaissance to map out the network topology, followed by resource development to establish command and control infrastructure. Initial access is gained through supply chain compromise, leading to execution of exploits on public-facing applications. Persistence is maintained via web shells, privilege escalation occurs through the use of valid accounts, defense evasion involves disabling security tools, and credential access is achieved by dumping operating system credentials. Discovery and lateral movement phases involve extensive network scanning and exploitation of remote services. Collection of sensitive data from repositories precedes exfiltration over a command and control channel. The final impact phase includes encrypting critical data to extort ransom.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, User Account, Windows Registry, Network Traffic, Target Artifact (Mobile device), Logon Session, Target Artifact (Channel)
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Application Log Content (Mechanism), Web Shell, User Account Authentication (Mechanism), Disabling Security Tools, Mimikatz Tool, Network Service Scanning, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Logon Session Creation (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, Data Encrypted for Impact

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers engage in active scanning activities to map the network topology, identifying potential entry points and vulnerabilities within the telecom infrastructure.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The adversaries acquire and setup command and control servers to facilitate communication and maintain persistence throughout the attack lifecycle.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2019-11707 (Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird type confusion vulnerability)
stage.description: By compromising a trusted third-party vendor, the attackers gain initial access to the target environment through a vulnerable application.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2019-16256 (SIMalliance Toolbox Browser command injection vulnerability)
stage.description: Exploiting known vulnerabilities in public-facing systems, the attackers execute malicious code to achieve their objectives.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: To maintain persistent access, the attackers plant web shells on compromised servers, enabling them to execute commands remotely.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Utilizing stolen credentials, the attackers escalate their privileges within the network to gain higher levels of control.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers impair defensive mechanisms by disabling security tools, reducing the likelihood of detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Using tools like Mimikatz, the attackers extract plaintext passwords and hashes from the memory of running systems.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers map the internal network structure to identify valuable resources for further lateral movement.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Employing remote service protocols, the attackers move laterally across the network to reach critical systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers systematically collect sensitive data from various repositories within the network.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is transferred securely to attacker-controlled servers using established command and control channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Finally, the attackers encrypt critical data to disrupt operations and coerce the victim into paying a ransom.

Scenario.summary: The complex cyber attack orchestrated by the expert-level threat group C0027 against the communications industry demonstrates a thorough understanding of the targeted environment and employs multiple sophisticated techniques to infiltrate, persist, and exfiltrate sensitive data while ensuring minimal detection and maximum impact.
